# dokploy_application_schedule (Resource)

A cron-scheduled command run inside a Dokploy application container.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "dokploy_application_schedule" "warmup" {
  application_id  = dokploy_application.api.id
  name            = "warmup-cache"
  cron_expression = "*/15 * * * *"
  command         = "curl -s http://localhost:3000/internal/warmup"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `application_id` (String) `dokploy_application.x.id`. Changing this forces replacement.
- `command` (String) Shell command to run.
- `cron_expression` (String) Cron expression (ex: `0 3 * * *`).
- `name` (String) Display name.

### Optional

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the schedule is active. Defaults to `true`.
- `shell_type` (String) Shell used to run the command. Defaults to `bash`.
- `timezone` (String) IANA timezone (ex: `America/Sao_Paulo`). Default null = UTC.

### Read-Only

- `app_name` (String) Internal name generated by Dokploy.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import dokploy_application_schedule.warmup <scheduleId>
```
